task_summary: Panel cohort cell: p1_compliance asks composer to build the schema_contracts_llm_assessment pipeline shape. Generated from panel-broad-2026-05-09.yaml on the panel-broad cohort.
opening_prompt:
I need to set up a workflow to have our medical case study assessments reviewed systematically. We have a CSV file with case studies — background information, symptoms, medical history — and we need each case to be evaluated against five criteria: diagnosis accuracy, treatment appropriateness, prognostic assessment, risk identification, and follow-up planning. Each evaluation should produce a score and a written rationale.

Now, before we proceed, I should mention a few constraints. Depending on how the tool handles errors — if one of the assessments fails to complete for whatever reason — we'll need to make sure those incomplete entries are set aside so they don't corrupt our evidentiary record. We need to keep both the successful assessments and the flagged entries, in case we need to revisit them for second-line review or audit purposes. We're also going to need a complete audit trail of what was processed, when, and what came back, because this will eventually be in scope for our controls testing.

The end result should be two outputs: a clean file with all the scores and rationales that I can forward to our medical team for their review, and a separate file flagging any cases that didn't complete properly so I can investigate those separately. 

Could you help me set that up?

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Lower-case substring match against final assistant content. The skill (anti-passivity section) explicitly forbids these phrases — any hit is RED. Standard list shared with the convergence suite.
red_criteria.build_failure_note: These strings are server-injected by service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message() when the model declares completion but the pipeline fails preflight. Their presence is definitive evidence the model failed to converge.
